gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Render parsed inline [`Span`]s to Typst markup: `#strong[…]`, `#emph[…]`, `#raw(\"…\")` for inline"] # [doc = " code, and `#link(\"…\")[…]` for **external** links only (internal/relative links render as their"] # [doc = " text — their resolved navigation is the `Xref` node, so the inline path never emits a broken PDF"] # [doc = " link to a raw `.md` path). Function forms are used throughout so code/link content with Typst"] # [doc = " metacharacters is robust (never a broken `.typ`)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Each inline **call** (`#raw`/`#strong`/`#emph`/`#link`) is followed by a zero-width space"] # [doc = " ([`CALL_SEP`]): in Typst markup a `(` or `[` immediately after a call is parsed as a *curried"] # [doc = " call* on its result (so a projected `` `math`(f64) `` → `#raw(\"math\")(f64)` would be a call, not"] # [doc = " text — a `typst compile` failure). The zero-width break terminates the call so the following char"] # [doc = " is literal text, while remaining invisible in the PDF (G2: every `.typ` compiles, output unchanged)."] fn render_inline_typst (spans : & [Span < '_ >]) -> String { let mut out = String :: new () ; for span in spans { match span { Span :: Text (t) => out . push_str (& escape (t)) , Span :: Code (c) => { out . push_str (& format ! ("#raw(\"{}\"){CALL_SEP}" , escape_str (c))) ; } Span :: Strong (inner) => { out . push_str ("#strong[") ; out . push_str (& render_inline_typst (inner)) ; out . push_str (& format ! ("]{CALL_SEP}")) ; } Span :: Em (inner) => { out . push_str ("#emph[") ; out . push_str (& render_inline_typst (inner)) ; out . push_str (& format ! ("]{CALL_SEP}")) ; } Span :: Link { text , href } => { if inline :: is_external (href) { out . push_str (& format ! ("#link(\"{}\")[" , escape_str (href))) ; out . push_str (& render_inline_typst (text)) ; out . push_str (& format ! ("]{CALL_SEP}")) ; } else { out . push_str (& render_inline_typst (text)) ; } } } } out }
gaps.reason: generic type path `Span < '_ >` — type argument `'_` is not a type (lifetime / const-generic / associated-type binding-or-constraint); `type_args` admits only type_refs, so left an explicit gap (VR-5)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The zero-width space appended after each inline Typst call to terminate a possible curried-call"] # [doc = " chain (see [`render_inline_typst`]). Invisible in the rendered PDF."] const CALL_SEP : & str = "\u{200b}" ;
gaps.reason: top-level `const CALL_SEP` — co-emit (ORACLE-R1 A4) only covers plain unsigned integer types (`u8`/`u16`/`u32`/`u64`/`u128`/`usize`); other types have no const item production and no faithful zero-arg-fn encoding (gap, never fabricate)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Parse + render inline markdown in `text` to Typst markup (the common one-shot)."] fn inline_typst (text : & str) -> String { render_inline_typst (& inline :: parse (text)) }
gaps.reason: qualified/associated-function call `inline :: parse` did not resolve to a known-emitted associated fn — Mycelium calls are bare identifiers, and the only sound surface form for `Type::method(...)` is the mangled `Type__method` name the declaration side already emits for a receiver-less inherent-impl associated fn (DN-34 §8.13/8.14), referenced only when that exact declaration is PROVEN present (same-file, or a resolved M-1084 cross-nodule sibling). A primitive/std associated fn with no emitted decl (e.g. `i128::try_from`), an unresolved type, or a `self`-receiving method (excluded by construction — it stays reachable only from its own bare call site) all gap here rather than fabricate a call (G2/VR-5, DN-133)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Render a whole prose block to Typst, **line by line**, so the emitted `.typ` always compiles."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Two robustness invariants, both never-silent (G2 — every `.typ` compiles):"] # [doc = " - **Per-line inline parsing.** Prose carries embedded newlines (soft wraps / the rows of a"] # [doc = "   projected pipe-table joined by the block parser). Parsing the *whole* block risks an unbalanced"] # [doc = "   backtick/marker pairing across lines (inverting text↔code and colliding Typst's `[]`/`\"\"`"] # [doc = "   delimiters). Parsing each line independently bounds any unbalanced marker to that one line"] # [doc = "   (where it degrades to literal text), so the delimiters stay balanced."] # [doc = " - **Leading-markup guard.** Typst reads block markup only at a LINE START — `/ ` (description-list"] # [doc = "   term), `- `/`+ ` (bullets), `= ` (heading), `N.`/`N)` (ordered list). A rendered line beginning"] # [doc = "   with one is prefixed with a zero-width `#h(0pt)` so the trigger is no longer at line start and"] # [doc = "   Typst reads it as literal text. Our own inline markup (`#strong[…]` etc.) starts with `#` — never"] # [doc = "   a trigger char — so it is left untouched."] fn render_prose_typst (text : & str) -> String { let mut out = String :: with_capacity (text . len () + 16) ; for (i , line) in text . split ('\n') . enumerate () { if i > 0 { out . push ('\n') ; } let rendered = inline_typst (line) ; if line_starts_block_markup (rendered . trim_start ()) { out . push_str ("#h(0pt)") ; } out . push_str (& rendered) ; } out }

A primitive/std associated fn with no emitted decl (e.g. `i128::try_from`), an unresolved type, or a `self`-receiving method (excluded by construction — it stays reachable only from its own bare call site) all gap here rather than fabricate a call (G2/VR-5, DN-133)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Whether a (leading-whitespace-trimmed) line begins with a Typst block-markup trigger."] fn line_starts_block_markup (t : & str) -> bool { let b = t . as_bytes () ; match b . first () { Some (b'/' | b'-' | b'+' | b'=') => true , Some (d) if d . is_ascii_digit () => { let end = b . iter () . take_while (| c | c . is_ascii_digit ()) . count () ; matches ! (b . get (end) , Some (b'.' | b')')) } _ => false , } }

Emitting bare `as_bytes(recv, …)` would fabricate an unknown prim and file-poison myc-check (G-β Rank A / G2/VR-5; residual when the method body itself gapped — e.g. M-1037 `.to_vec()` inside `read_to_end` — so the declaration was never registered)

gaps.snippet:
# [doc = " Render the whole model to one Typst document source."] # [must_use] pub fn render (model : & DocModel) -> String { let mut out = String :: new () ; out . push_str ("// Generated from the Mycelium corpus — a projection, never a parallel truth (ADR-003/G11).
\
         // Compile with: typst compile doc.typ doc.pdf  (skipped gracefully when typst is absent).
\
         #set document(title: "Mycelium Documentation")
\
         #set page(numbering: "1", margin: (x: 2.2cm, y: 2.4cm))
\
         #set text(font: "New Computer Modern", size: 10.5pt)
\
         #set heading(numbering: "1.1")
\
         // Print-legible code (§8.2): a light tinted box with a HAIRLINE border (never a filled dark
\
         // panel), code ~0.82x body with tighter leading so lines fit without wrapping. Light-only.
\
         #show raw.where(block: true): it => {
\
         set text(size: 8.6pt)
\
         set par(leading: 0.42em)
\
         block(fill: rgb("#e4e9d9"), stroke: 0.5pt + rgb("#cfd5c1"), radius: 3pt, inset: (x: 8pt, y: 7pt), width: 100%, breakable: true, it)
\
         }

\
         #align(center)[#text(18pt)[*Mycelium Documentation*]]
\
         #align(center)[_A projection of the cited corpus._]

\
         #outline()

" ,) ; for doc in & model . documents { render_doc (doc , & mut out) ; } out }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Escape Typst markup metacharacters in body text. `pub(crate)` for white-box unit testing in"] # [doc = " `src/tests/typst.rs`, not a downstream API."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `[`/`]` are escaped too: content flows into `#strong[…]`/`#emph[…]` **content arguments**, so an"] # [doc = " unescaped `]` in the text would close the bracket early and break the `.typ`. (Inside `#raw(\"…\")`"] # [doc = " / `#link(\"…\")` string arguments the delimiter is a quote, handled by [`escape_str`].)"] pub (crate) fn escape (s : & str) -> String { let mut out = String :: with_capacity (s . len ()) ; for ch in s . chars () { match ch { '#' | '$' | '*' | '_' | '`' | '<' | '>' | '@' | '\\' | '[' | ']' => { out . push ('\\') ; out . push (ch) ; } _ => out . push (ch) , } } out }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Escape for a Typst **string literal** (used inside `#link(\"…\")` and `#raw(\"…\")`). A Typst string"] # [doc = " cannot contain a raw newline (or other control char), so those are escaped too — otherwise a"] # [doc = " projected inline-code span that captured a newline (e.g. an unbalanced backtick across the rows of"] # [doc = " a projected pipe-table) would terminate the string early and break the whole `.typ`. Escaping"] # [doc = " `\\`, `\"`, and the C0 controls makes ANY content a valid single-line string literal (G2: every"] # [doc = " emitted `.typ` compiles)."] fn escape_str (s : & str) -> String { let mut out = String :: with_capacity (s . len () + 2) ; for ch in s . chars () { match ch { '\\' => out . push_str ("\\\\") , '"' => out . push_str ("\\\"") , '\n' => out . push_str ("\\n") , '\r' => out . push_str ("\\r") , '\t' => out . push_str ("\\t") , c if (c as u32) < 0x20 => out . push_str (& format ! ("\\u{{{:x}}}" , c as u32)) , c => out . push (c) , } } out }
